title: Hurricane Ida — Category 4 Gulf Coast landfall
description: Hurricane Ida makes landfall near Leeville, Louisiana on August 29, 2021 as a Category 4 storm with 150 mph winds — the strongest storm to hit Louisiana since the 1850s. It knocks out power to 1M+ customers, shuts nearly all Gulf of Mexico oil and gas production, closes the ExxonMobil Baton Rouge refinery (330k bbl/day), and causes $65B+ in insured losses.
